(Recommended when the environment has only one worker.)</p></div><div><p>You can use this option with:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CloudHub 1.0, CloudHub 2.0, and Runtime Fabric deployments with one worker or replica</p></li><li><p>Hybrid standalone deployments, including clustered Mule runtimes</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p><code>OBJECT_STORE</code></p><div><p>The connector always uses an alternative mechanism in environments in which the runtimeâs distributed locking mechanism doesnât work. (Recommended when the environment has more than one worker.)</p></div><div><p>You can use this option with:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CloudHub 1.0, CloudHub 2.0, and Runtime Fabric deployments, including multiple workers or replicas</p></li><li><p>Hybrid standalone deployments, including clustered Mule runtimes</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include ignored segments</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to include data elements from segments with <code>Ignore</code> as the usage type in the output map and metadata.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If <code>true</code>, all segments with <code>Ignore</code> as the usage type are included in both the output map and metadata.</p></li><li><p>If <code>false</code>, all segments with <code>Ignore</code> as the usage type are ignored in both the output map and metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Associated Operations

- Read
- Write
- Write batch

## Read

`<x12:read>`

Transforms an input stream of EDI text into the maps and lists structure of the EDI data.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Input payload.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- X12:UNKNOWN
- X12:WRITE
- X12:SCHEMA
- X12:PARSE

## Write

`<x12:write>`

Transforms the maps and lists structure of EDI data into an EDI text stream.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Write Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Output payload.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable in memory stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable file store stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non repeatable stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams.</p></td><td><p>Repeatable file store stream</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- X12:UNKNOWN
- X12:WRITE
- X12:SCHEMA
- X12:PARSE

## Write batch

`<x12:write-batch>`

Merges individual X12 messages created and accumulated over a period of time into a single
interchange (UNB).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Write Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Output payload.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- config

### Throws

- X12:UNKNOWN
- X12:WRITE
- X12:SCHEMA
- X12:PARSE

## Expiration Policy Type

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Repeatable In Memory Stream Type

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the **Max
Buffer Size** field value. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your
specific use case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong> fields.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Repeatable File Store Stream Type

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum memory size. If more memory is consumed, then the connector buffers the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit for the <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## System Properties

You can configure the following system property for the connector:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>strictValidationOfYear</code></p></td><td><p>Enables strict validation for the year <code>0000</code>. If <code>true</code>, the year <code>0000</code> is invalid.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For more information about system properties, refer to
[System Properties](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-system).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Use Anypoint Studio to Configure X12 EDI Connector 2.18

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

You must change the default Anypoint configuration to use X12 Connector 2.x.

## Increasing Anypoint Studio 7.x Memory

If more than the standard memory configuration is required to use Anypoint Studio 7.x with X12
Connector 2.x, then youâll need to change the memory setting for Studio.

Set the Anypoint Studio memory to 4 GB, as this is sufficient for the most common uses of this
connector.

1.  Edit the `Anypoint.ini` file in the Anypoint Studio installation directory and change the two
    lines starting with `-Xms` and `-Xmx` to:

```text
-Xms4096m
-Xmx4096m
```

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Use Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio). If,
after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[X12 EDI Reference](x12-edi-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add X12 EDI (X12 Connector) to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `X12 EDI` in the search field.
3.  Click X12 Connector in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure this source to use
with X12 EDI Connector:

- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port.

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for X12 EDI Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **X12 EDI** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target X12 EDI system.

To configure the global element for X12 EDI (X12 EDI Connector), follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.

You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders (recommended),
or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration properties.

For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure them, see
[Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors).

1.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
2.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
3.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for X12 EDI (X12 EDI Connector), configure the other required
fields for the connector.

### General Tab

Use the **General** tab to configure settings for reading and writing X12 messages.

> [!NOTE] To use the Functional Acknowledgment schema type (997 or 999) it must match the **Generate
> 999 Functional Acknowledgments** setting on the **Parser** tab (`false` for 997, `true` for 999).

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Form and validation</p></td><td><p>X12 form and validation (standard X12, HIPAA SNIP Type 1, or HIPAA SNIP Type 2)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema definitions</p></td><td><p>Manually create or edit the list of schema definitions for the message structures to use. They all need to use the same form (either X12 or HIPAA).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding</p></td><td><p>X12 character encoding, used for both send and receive messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character set</p></td><td><p>X12 character set allowed in string data (BASIC, EXTENDED, or UNRESTRICTED)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Substitution character</p></td><td><p>String substitution character, used to replace invalid characters in string data</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version identifier suffix</p></td><td><p>Version identifier code suffix, specifying a value appended to the base schema version in the GS08 Version / Release / Industry Identifier Code field</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Identity Tab

Use the **Identity** tab to configure the Interchange Control Header (ISA) and Functional Group
Header (GS) identifier values for you and your trading partner in X12 Connector configuration. If
configured, then the values are verified when reading an input message and used as the defaults when
writing an output message (if no value output data is specified).

You can set the values for the:

- Interchange ID qualifier and Interchange ID for your Mule application and partner
- Application Code for Mule and partner

The Mule values specify the Receiver fields (ISA07/ISA08, GS03) in a receive message and populate
the Sender fields (ISA05/ISA06, GS02) fields in a send message.

### Parser Tab

You can set the following options to control parser validation of receive messages if needed.
Changing these options from the default setting of true interferes with HIPAA SNIP Type 1 and Type 2
validation of messages. Use these options with standard X12 data, not with HIPAA.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enforce length limits</p></td><td><p>Enforce minimum and maximum lengths for receive values</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce character set</p></td><td><p>Enforce allowed character set</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce value repeats</p></td><td><p>Enforce repetition count limits for receive values</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allow unknown segments</p></td><td><p>Allow unknown segments in a transaction set</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce segment order</p></td><td><p>Enforce segment order in a transaction set</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allow unused segments</p></td><td><p>Allow segments marked as Unused in a transaction set</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce segment repeats</p></td><td><p>Enforce segment repetition count limits in a transaction set</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Require unique interchanges</p></td><td><p>Require unique ISA Interchange Control Numbers (ISA13)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Require unique groups</p></td><td><p>Enforce globally unique Group Control Numbers (GS06) for received functional groups</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Require unique transactions</p></td><td><p>Enforce globally unique Transaction Set Control Numbers (ST02) for received transaction sets</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store time-to-live</p></td><td><p>Set the minimum number of days to store interchange, group, and transaction set numbers for checking uniqueness</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledge every transaction</p></td><td><p>Include a separate AK2/AK5 (997) or AK2/IK5 (999) acknowledgment for every received transaction set. Instead of using implicit acknowledgments for transaction sets without errors</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Generate 999 acknowledgments</p></td><td><p>Generate 999 Implementation Acknowledgments, rather than the 997 Functional Acknowledgments default. The support for 999 Implementation Acknowledgments does not include CTX segment generation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Report segment details</p></td><td><p>Report segment error details to sender in the generated 997 or 999 acknowledgments</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Acknowledgement schema</p></td><td><p>Automatically include a standard 997 or 999 schema that matches the generated acknowledgment in the configuration for received messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledgment schema path</p></td><td><p>Set the path to a Functional Acknowledgment schema to be used for generated acknowledgments such as <code>/hipaa/005010X231A1/999.esl</code>, that uses the standard HIPAA 005010X231A1 acknowledgment. The schema type included in the path must match the Generate 999 Functional Acknowledgments setting (<code>false</code> for 997, <code>true</code> for 999). If you do not set the path, the parser uses a hardcoded X12 997 or 999 schema, as appropriate.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce conditional rules</p></td><td><p>Enforce conditional rules for receive values</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Writer Tab

Writer options let you specify the separator characters used when writing messages.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Data element separator</p></td><td><p>Specify characters when writing messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Component element separator</p></td><td><p>Specify characters when writing messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Repetition separator</p></td><td><p>Specify characters when writing messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment terminator</p></td><td><p>Specify characters when writing messages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment line ending</p></td><td><p>Line ending written between segments, used to make the output more readable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unique group numbers</p></td><td><p>Whether to generate unique GS group control numbers across interchanges</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unique transaction numbers</p></td><td><p>Whether to generate unique ST transaction set control numbers across groups and interchanges</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Implementation reference</p></td><td><p>An implementation convention reference (ST03) value</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial interchange number</p></td><td><p>The starting interchange control number. Only used when no current value is found in the object store</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial group number</p></td><td><p>The starting group control number. Only used when no current value is found in the object store</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial transaction number</p></td><td><p>The starting transaction set control number. Only used when no current value is found in the object store</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request acknowledgments</p></td><td><p>Whether to request interchange acknowledgments (ISA14)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default usage indicator</p></td><td><p>The default interchange usage indicator (ISA15). Used when no value is present in the message data</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use supplied values</p></td><td><p>Whether to use supplied values for control segment information (control numbers, in particular). The default behavior is always generate control numbers when writing. This allows you to use chosen values</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output mime type</p></td><td><p>Whether to use the default output MIME type <code>application/plain</code>, or the X12-specific alternative <code>application/edi-x12</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce length limits</p></td><td><p>Whether to enforce length limits on input data</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce conditional rules</p></td><td><p>Whether to enforce conditional rules on input data. The default is to ignore conditional rules</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Interchange number key</p></td><td><p>The key-value pair of the interchange control key and interchange control number</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group number key</p></td><td><p>The key-value pair of group control key and group control number</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction number key</p></td><td><p>The key-value pair of the transaction control key and transaction control number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, configure the other fields for the
connector. See [Additional Configuration Information](x12-edi-connector-config-topics) for more
configuration steps.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# X12 EDI Connector 2.18 - Understanding the EDI Schema Language

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

X12 EDI Connector uses a YAML format called EDI Schema Language (ESL) to represent EDI schemas.
Basic ESLs define the structure of Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages in terms of:

- structures, known as _transaction sets_ in X12 terminology
- groups, known as loops, in X12
- segments, composites, and elements

ESLs for the all [supported X12 versions](x12-edi-versions-x12) are included. If you are using
HIPAA, then a set of [HIPAA variations](x12-edi-versions-hipaa) of base X12 transaction sets are
included.

You can customize X12 schema definitions to suit your data by defining an overlay schema. An overlay
schema is a type of ESL that enables you to modify a base schema, such as an X12 4010 850 schema,
with your specific conventions. You donât need an overlay schema if youâre using the structure
defined by the standard, but most EDI exchanges modify the base definitions in an implementation
convention.

You can define your own custom schemas, or copy and edit a base X12 schema definition to suit your
data as shown in the [EDI Schema Language Reference](x12-edi-schema-language-reference).

## Finding Schema Locations

To use the connector, you need to know the locations of the schemas in your project. If youâre using
out-of-the-box X12 schemas and not customizing anything, the schema location follows the
`/x12/{version}/{transaction set}.esl` convention.

For example, using the `005010` version and the 850 transaction set, the schema location is
`/x12/005010/850.esl`.

If youâre using one or more custom schemas, refer to
[XML Schema and Trading Partner Configuration](x12-edi-connector-config-topics) for complete
information about setting your schema locations.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# X12 EDI Connector 2.18 - Message Structure Hierarchy

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use this connector to read or write X12 documents into (or from) the canonical EDI message
structure. This structure is a hierarchy of Java Maps and Lists. You use either DataWeave or code to
manipulate them. Each transaction structure is defined in the schemas.

The message itself contains the following keys, some of which apply only to either the read
operation or the write operation, as indicated:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delimiters</p></td><td><p>An optional set of delimiters used in the message. If used in a read operation, the delimiters are based on the most recent interchange processed by a read operation. If used for a write operation, the delimiters override the values in the module configuration. The characters in the string of delimiters are interpreted based on their position in string, in the following order: data separator, subelement separator, repetition separator, and segment terminator, with a value of <code>U</code> indicating no value in that position.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Errors (read only)</p></td><td><p>A list of errors that are associated either with the input as a whole or with interchanges that have no valid transaction sets.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FunctionalAcksGenerated (read only)</p></td><td><p>A list of <code>997</code> or <code>999</code> configured acknowledgments that are generated by the module during the read operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group (write only)</p></td><td><p>A map of GS group header segment values used as defaults when writing groups</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Interchange (write only)</p></td><td><p>A map of ISA interchange header segment values used as defaults during an interchange write operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>InterchangeAcksGenerated (read only)</p></td><td><p>A list of TA1 acknowledgments that were generated by the module during the read operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>InterchangeAcksReceived (read only)</p></td><td><p>A list of TA1 acknowledgments that were received by the module during the read operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>InterchangeAcksToSend (write only)</p></td><td><p>A list of TA1 acknowledgments that are sent by the module during the write operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactions</p></td><td><p>A hierarchy of the transaction sets that are either read by the module during a read operation or sent by the module during a write operation. The value of this top-level key is a map with standard versions as keys, in the form of <code>v005010</code>. For example, if you are using version <code>005010</code> with <code>850</code> and <code>855</code> transaction sets, the transactions contain a map with one key, <code>v005010</code>. The value of this key is another map, one with two keys, <code>850</code> and <code>855</code>. Each of these contain a list of individual <code>850</code> and <code>855</code> transaction sets that are processed. If youâre using <code>997</code> functional acknowledgments, then <code>997</code> is the key for a list of 997 transaction sets.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Individual transaction sets have their own maps, with the following keys:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Detail</p></td><td><p>A map of segments or loops from the detail section of the transaction set. Values are maps for segments or loops that occur once. Lists of maps exist for segments or loops that occur more than once.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Errors (read only)</p></td><td><p>A list of errors that are associated either with the input as a whole or with interchanges that have no valid transaction sets.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group</p></td><td><p>A map containing GS group header segment values. For a read message, the map is the actual data from the enclosing group. It is a single map linked from all transaction sets in the group. For a write message, the map value creates the enclosing group. If there are no values for this map, then the default is the value(s) taken from the Group map at the message level.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Heading</p></td><td><p>A map of segments or loops from the heading section of the transaction set. Values are maps for segments or loops that occur once. Lists of maps exist for values that occur more than once.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id</p></td><td><p>The Transaction ID must match the key of the containing transaction list.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Interchange</p></td><td><p>A map of ISA interchange header segment values. For a read message, the map is the actual data from the enclosing interchange. It is a single map linked from all transaction sets in the interchange. For a write message, the map value creates the enclosing interchange. For example, gathering transactions with the same interchange values into a single interchange, regardless of whether the actual maps are the same. If there are no values for this map, then the default is the value(s) taken from the Interchange map at the message level.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Transaction set name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SetHeader</p></td><td><p>A map of ST transaction set header segment values. This provides the actual header data for a Read operation, and enables you to provide overrides for Write operation configuration settings.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Summary</p></td><td><p>A map of segments or loops from the summary section of the transaction set. These values are maps for segments or loops that occur once. The list of maps are for values that are repeated.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Generated `997` or `999` functional acknowledgment transactions differ from received messages in
their handling of interchange information:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Interchange</p></td><td><p>A map of ISA interchange header segment values. For functional acknowledgments generated by receive processing, this map is a copy of the data for the containing interchange with sender and receiver identification components (ISA05/ISA06 and ISA07/ISA08) interchanged. When writing a message, these values are used for creating the enclosing interchange. For example, gathering transactions with the same interchange values into a single interchange, regardless of whether the actual maps are the same. If there are no values for this map, then the default is the value(s) taken from the Interchange map at the message level.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

TA1 interchange acknowledgments are in the form of maps representing the TA1 segment data and linked
to data on the corresponding interchange:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Interchange</p></td><td><p>A map of ISA interchange header segment values. For TA1 acknowledgments generated by receive processing, this map is a copy of the data for the interchange matching the TA1 with sender and receiver identification components (ISA05/ISA06 and ISA07/ISA08) interchanged. When writing a message, these values are used for creating the enclosing interchange. It gathers transactions with the same interchange values into a single interchange, regardless of whether the actual maps are the same. If there are no values for this map, then the default is the value(s) are taken from the Interchange map at the message level.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# X12 EDI Connector 2.18 - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/x12-edi
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/x12-edi/current/mule-x12-edi.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:x12-edi="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/x12-edi"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/x12-edi
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-x12-connector</artifactId>
  <version>2.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace the <version> with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `X12 Connector`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](x12-edi-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# X12 EDI Connector 2.18 - EDI Schema Language Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Anypoint B2B Connectors use a YAML format called EDI Schema Language (ESL) to represent EDI
schemas. Basic ESLs define the composition of EDI messages with structures, segments, composites,
and elements. ESL supports the following Connectors:

- Anypoint Connector for X12 EDI (X12 EDI Connector)
- Anypoint Connector for EDIFACT (EDIFACT Connector)
- Anypoint Connector for TRADACOMS (TRADACOMS Connector)
- Anypoint Connector for HL7 (HL7 Connector)

## Components of an ESL Document

The top-level definitions in an ESL document show:

- the EDI standard
- the version of that standard
- an optional list of import ESLs
- any combination structures, segments, composites, and element definitions.

The following example shows what these top-level definitions look like from an `/x12/005010/850.esl`
schema:

```yaml
form: X12
version: '005010'
imports: [ '/x12/005010/basedefs.esl' ]
structures:
- id: '850'
  name: Purchase Order
  class: PO
  ...
```

There is an ESL for each X12 and EDIFACT version named `basedefs.esl`, providing the standard
segment, composite, and element definitions. This example shows an 850 purchase order that imports
these to reuse the definitions representing the purchase order structure.

### Readability

YAML uses a combination of lists and sets of key-value pairs. The order of values is not important,
as long as the required items are present. Quotes (such as single or double) are used around values
that consist of digits but are meant to be interpreted as strings (otherwise the YAML parser treats
the values as numbers). Indentation is used to show the nesting of lists.

The ESL structures shown here define all simple key-value pairs before any lists that are part of
the same definition.

## Structure Definitions

These represent X12 transaction sets or EDIFACT messages and are composed of key-value pairs for
standard characteristics with lists of segments that comprise the actual structure. Segment lists
are organized into heading, detail, and summary sections. These can be further refined into groups
consisting of a potentially repeated sequence of segments.

This example shows a sample structure definition from the `/x12/005010/850.esl` schema:

```yaml
- id: '850'
  name: Purchase Order
  class: PO
  heading:
  - { idRef: 'ST', position: '0100', usage: M }
  - { idRef: 'BEG', position: '0200', usage: M }
  - { idRef: 'CUR', position: '0400', usage: O }
  - { idRef: 'REF', position: '0500', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - { idRef: 'PER', position: '0600', usage: O, count: 3 }
  - { idRef: 'TAX', position: '0700', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - { idRef: 'FOB', position: '0800', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - { idRef: 'CTP', position: '0900', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - { idRef: 'PAM', position: '0950', usage: O, count: 10 }
  - { idRef: 'CSH', position: '1100', usage: O, count: 5 }
  - { idRef: 'TC2', position: '1150', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - groupId: 'SAC'
    usage: O
    count: 25
    items:
    - { idRef: 'SAC', position: '1200', usage: O }
    - { idRef: 'CUR', position: '1250', usage: O }
  - { idRef: 'ITD', position: '1300', usage: O, count: '>1' }
  - { idRef: 'DIS', position: '1400', usage: O, count: 20 }
  ...
```

The basic structure values are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Structure Key/Section</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Structure identifier</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Structure name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>class</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A class of structure that is equivalent to an X12 functional group identifier. This is ignored in EDIFACT structure definitions.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>heading</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segments and groups within the heading section of the structure.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>detail</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segments and groups within the detail section of the structure.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>summary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segments and groups within the heading section of the structure.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The lists of segments for each section of the structure use the same form. Each item in the list is
either a segment reference or a group definition. Segment references use a compact YAML syntax: the
values for each are shown as comma-separated key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces.

The values are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Segment Property</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The referenced segment ID.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The segment position within the section. With the EDI convention these are numeric values that can include leading zeros, so these are quoted and handled as strings.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code definition:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>C</code> for Conditional</p></li><li><p><code>M</code> for Mandatory</p></li><li><p><code>O</code> for Optional</p></li><li><p><code>U</code> for Unused, the item should not appear at all in the data.</p></li><li><p><code>I</code> for Ignored, this can be present in data, as in the case of the HL segments it must be present, but is not visible for the user in metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value that can be a number or the special value &gt;1 . This means any number of repeats. A count value of 1 is used if not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Group definitions are shown in expanded form, with key-value pairs on separate lines. The values in
a group definition are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>groupId</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The group identifier</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code definition:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>C</code> for Conditional</p></li><li><p><code>M</code> for Mandatory</p></li><li><p><code>O</code> for Optional</p></li><li><p><code>U</code> for Unused, the item should not appear at all in the data.</p></li><li><p><code>I</code> for Ignored, this can be present in data, as in the case of the HL segments it must be present, but is not visible for the user in metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value that can be a number or the special value &gt;1 . This means any number of repeats. A count value of 1 is used if not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>items</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segments and potentially nested groups that comprise the group.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Segment Definitions

Segment definitions are comprised of certain key-value pairs for standard characteristics along with
lists of values such as elements and composites that make up the actual segment. The following
example shows a portion of a sample segment definition, from the `/x12/005010/basedefs.esl` schema

`M` is mandatory `O` is Optional

```yaml
- id: "BAK"
  name: Beginning Segment for Purchase Order Acknowledgment
  values:
    - { idRef: "353", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "587", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "324", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "373", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "328", usage: O }
    - { idRef: "326", usage: O }
```

Segment definition values are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Section</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>segment identifier</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>segment name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>values</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>list of elements and composites within the segment</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The values list references elements and composites by ID, and use a compact YAML syntax: the values
for each are shown as comma-separated key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Section</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The referenced element or composite ID.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The value position within the segment starts at 1 and increases by 1 for each successive value. Generally not used.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the value in the segment, by default the element or composite name is used.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code definition:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>C</code> for Conditional</p></li><li><p><code>M</code> for Mandatory</p></li><li><p><code>O</code> for Optional</p></li><li><p><code>U</code> for Unused, the item should not appear at all in the data.</p></li><li><p><code>I</code> for Ignored, this can be present in data, as in the case of the HL segments it must be present, but is not visible for the user in metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value that can be a number or the special value &gt;1 . This means any number of repeats. A count value of 1 is used if not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Composite Definitions

Composite definitions are very similar to segment definitions. They are composed of certain
key-value pairs for standard characteristics along with lists of values such as elements and
composites that make up the actual composite. This example shows a portion of a composite
definition, from the `/x12/005010/basedefs.esl schema`.

```yaml
- id: "C022"
  name: "Health Care Code Information"
  values:
    - { idRef: "1270", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "1271", usage: M }
    - { idRef: "1250", usage: C }
    - { idRef: "1251", usage: C }
    - { idRef: "782", usage: O }
```

Composite definition values are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>composite identifier</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>composite name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>values</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>list of elements and composites within the composite</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The values list references elements and composites by ID, and use a compact YAML syntax: the values
for each are shown as comma-separated key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The referenced element or composite ID</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The value position within the segment, starts at 1 and increases by 1 for each successive value. Generally not used.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code definition:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>C</code> for Conditional</p></li><li><p><code>M</code> for Mandatory</p></li><li><p><code>O</code> for Optional</p></li><li><p><code>U</code> for Unused, the item should not appear at all in the data.</p></li><li><p><code>I</code> for Ignored, this can be present in data, as in the case of the HL segments it must be present, but is not visible for the user in metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Element Definitions

Element definitions are simple with only basic key-value pairs for standard characteristics.

This example shows this from the `/x12/005010/basedefs.esl` schema:

```yaml
elements:
  - { id: "1", name: "Route Code", type: AN, minLength: 1, maxLength: 13 }
  - { id: "100", name: "Currency Code", type: ID, minLength: 3, maxLength: 3 }
  - { id: "1000", name: "Service Characteristics Qualifier", type: AN, minLength: 2, maxLength: 3 }
```

Element definition values are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Element identifier</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Element name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value type code (Binary data type is not currently supported):</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>N</code> for an integer number</p></li><li><p><code>N0-N9</code> for a number with an implied decimal point at the indicated position (N0 is equivalent to N)</p></li><li><p><code>R</code> for a decimal number</p></li><li><p><code>ID</code> for an identifier</p></li><li><p><code>AN</code> for an alphanumeric string</p></li><li><p><code>DT</code> for a date</p></li><li><p><code>TM</code> for a time</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>minLength</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum number of significant characters in the value.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>maxLength</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of significant characters in the value.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Specify a Schema According to Your Implementation Convention

You can define your implementation convention with an Overlay Schema. Overlay schemas specify how to
use implementation conventions with a particular trading partner to extend and customizes the
standard. These are very similar in structure to complete schemas but instead of providing all the
details of the schema structure they only list changes.

You create an overlay schema with the following process:

1.  Create an overlay schema that imports the base schema you want to customize, for example,
    `X12 005010 850`.
2.  Customize the overall structure for segment usage, positions, groups, and counts.
3.  Customize segments, including usage and counts.

The following example shows a portion of a sample overlay schema that modifies the basic
`X12 005010 850` transaction set definition. This example customizes the `CUR` segment and specifies
that it is unused, thereby ensuring it is hidden from the mapping structures in Anypoint Studio.

```yaml
form: X12
version: "005010"
imports: ["/x12/005010/850.esl"]
structures:
  - idRef: "850"
    name: Purchase Order
    class: PO
    heading:
      - { idRef: "CUR", position: "0400", usage: U }
```

### Structure Overlay

A structure overlay details modifications to the base schema definition of an X12 transaction set.
Most often these modifications take the form of marking segments or groups in the base definition as
unused, but any usage or repetition count change is allowed.

The modifications in this example specify that the `CUR` and `PER` segments of the standard `850`
heading are not being used, along with the segments in the `N9` loop.

```yaml
- idRef: "850"
  heading:
    - { idRef: "CUR", position: "0400", usage: U }
    - { idRef: "PER", position: "0600", usage: U }
    - groupIdRef: "N9_Loop"
      position: "2950"
      items:
        - { idRef: "DTM", position: "2970", usage: U }
        - { idRef: "PWK", position: "3050", usage: U }
        - { idRef: "EFI", position: "3080", usage: U }
```

The key-value pairs at the structure level are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID for the transaction set being modified.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The transaction set name, if used.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>heading, detail, summary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segment and group modifications within each section of the structure. If used, each is only used when there are modifications to that section.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The lists of segment modifications for the different sections of the structure such as heading,
detail, summary all use the same structure. Each item in the list is either a segment reference or a
group definition. Segment references are shown using a compact YAML syntax where the values for each
reference are given as comma-separated key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The referenced segment ID. Verified if provided, but otherwise ignored. The position value is used to uniquely identify segments within the section.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The segment position within the transaction set section.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code, <code>M</code> for Mandatory, <code>O</code> for Optional, <code>C</code> for Conditional, or <code>U</code> for Unused.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value, which may be a number or the special value <code>&gt;1</code> meaning any number of repeats. The count value, if specified. Uses a base definition value if not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Group overlays are shown in expanded form, with key-value pairs on separate lines.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>groupIdRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The referenced group ID. Verified if provided, but otherwise ignored. The position value is used to uniquely identify a group within a section.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The segment position within the transaction set section.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code, <code>M</code> for Mandatory, <code>O</code> for Optional, <code>C</code> for Conditional, or <code>U</code> for Unused.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value that can be a number or the special value &gt;1 . This means any number of repeats. The count value and a base definition value is used if a value is not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>items</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of segments and potentially nested loops making up the loop.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Segment Overlays

A segment overlay delineates modifications to the base schema definition. These modifications can
take the form of marking elements or composites in the base definition as unused. Any usage or
repetition count change is allowed. The following example uses the compact form for segment
modifications that only involve a truncation, while modifications that make changes to individual
values are expressed in expanded form. As with all the other YAML examples, the two forms are
actually equivalent and are used interchangeably.

```yaml
segments:
  - { idRef: AMT, trim: 3 }
  - idRef: BEG
    values:
      - { position: 4, usage: U }
  - { idRef: DTM, trim: 3 }
  - idRef: ITD
    values:
      - { position: 4, usage: U }
      - { position: 6, usage: U }
```

> [!NOTE] Segment overlays do not automatically apply to all uses of a segment, they are only
> effective for segments referenced within a structure overlay. The structure overlay doesnât need
> to make any changes to the usage of the segment, but must reference the segment at the appropriate
> position(s) so that the segment overlay is used to modify the base definition.

The key-value pairs in a segment overlay.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>idRef</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Segment identifier.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>trim</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Trim position in segment, if used, all values from this point on are marked as unused.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>values</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of individual value modifications.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The values list references values in the segment by position. The key-value pairs for these
references.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>position</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The value position within the segment.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the value in the segment, if used. The base definition value used if not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>usage</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Usage code, <code>M</code> for Mandatory, <code>O</code> for Optional, <code>C</code> for Conditional, or <code>U</code> for Unused.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum repetition count value, which may be any number or the special value <code>&gt;1</code> meaning any number of repeats. The count value and a base definition value is used if a value is not specified.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# X12 EDI Connector 2.18 - X12 EDI HIPAA Versions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following lists the transaction sets supported by the Mule X12 EDI HIPAA connector for each
version of the X12 standard.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>X12 Version</th><th>Supported Transaction Sets</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>005010X210</p></td><td><p>275</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X212</p></td><td><p>276 277</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X214</p></td><td><p>277</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X215</p></td><td><p>278RQ, 278RS</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X216</p></td><td><p>278RQ, 278RS</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X217</p></td><td><p>278RQ, 278RS</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X218</p></td><td><p>820</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X220A1</p></td><td><p>834</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X221A1</p></td><td><p>835</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X222A1</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X222A2</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X223A2</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X223A3</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X224A2</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X224A3</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X228</p></td><td><p>277</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X231A1</p></td><td><p>999</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X279A1</p></td><td><p>270 271</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X298</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X299</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X300</p></td><td><p>837</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>005010X306</p></td><td><p>820</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Version 004040 VICS
